_note: Link's Awakening (game 6, Game Boy 1993) static bundle fields. assemble-la.mjs merges these with walkthrough.json (REGIONS) into app-data.json. Empty datasets degrade gracefully like TotK/OoT/MM v1. This game is set on KOHOLINT, not Hyrule — there is no Triforce and no Ganon. The whole island is the dream of the sleeping Wind Fish; to go home, Link gathers the 8 Instruments of the Sirens (the 'Stones & Medallions' analog) and plays the Ballad of the Wind Fish to wake it. Bittersweet by design.

RUNES.name: Sword
RUNES.from: Lying on the south beach where Marin found you — your first task is to get it back.
RUNES.what: Your main weapon. At full health it shoots a sword beam, and you can later upgrade it to the L-2 Sword at the Seashell Mansion.
RUNES.tip: You can only hold two items at once (A and B buttons), so the Sword usually shares your hands with Roc's Feather early on.

RUNES.name: Roc's Feather
RUNES.from: Found in Tail Cave, the first dungeon.
RUNES.what: Lets Link jump — over pits, spikes, and small gaps. Pair it with the Pegasus Boots to long-jump.
RUNES.tip: Jumping is core to nearly every dungeon here. Feather + Boots together clears gaps neither can alone.

RUNES.name: Power Bracelet
RUNES.from: Found in Bottle Grotto, the second dungeon.
RUNES.what: Lets you lift pots, skulls, and rocks and hurl them at enemies and switches.
RUNES.tip: Many blocked paths are just a liftable rock away once you have this.

RUNES.name: Hookshot
RUNES.from: Found in Catfish's Maw, the fifth dungeon.
RUNES.what: Fires a chain that grabs distant blocks to pull you across pits, and stuns or drags enemies.
RUNES.tip: A late-but-mighty tool — it trivializes several earlier rooms if you revisit them.

RUNES.name: Ocarina
RUNES.from: Won from a trade / found in the village — you learn three songs for it.
RUNES.what: Plays three melodies: the Ballad of the Wind Fish (the goal song), Manbo's Mambo (a warp), and the Frog's Song of Soul.
RUNES.tip: The Ballad of the Wind Fish is needed to calm Marin and to finish the game. Guard it.

CHAMPIONS.name: Full Moon Cello
CHAMPIONS.note: The first Instrument of the Sirens, won from Tail Cave beneath the eastern hills.

CHAMPIONS.name: Conch Horn
CHAMPIONS.note: The second Instrument, won from Bottle Grotto in the Goponga Swamp area.

CHAMPIONS.name: Sea Lily's Bell
CHAMPIONS.note: The third Instrument, won from the Key Cavern near Ukuku Prairie.

CHAMPIONS.name: Surf Harp
CHAMPIONS.note: The fourth Instrument, won from Angler's Tunnel inside Tal Tal Heights.

CHAMPIONS.name: Wind Marimba
CHAMPIONS.note: The fifth Instrument, won from Catfish's Maw on the eastern coast.

CHAMPIONS.name: Coral Triangle
CHAMPIONS.note: The sixth Instrument, won from the Face Shrine in the eastern desert.

CHAMPIONS.name: Organ of Evening Calm
CHAMPIONS.note: The seventh Instrument, won from Eagle's Tower high on Tal Tal Mountains.

CHAMPIONS.name: Thunder Drum
CHAMPIONS.note: The eighth and final Instrument, won from Turtle Rock at the mountain's peak.

ROADMAP.name: The Eight Instruments
ROADMAP.note: Each of the island's eight dungeons holds one Instrument of the Sirens. Play all eight together at the Egg atop Mt. Tamaranch to rouse the sleeping Wind Fish — and end the dream.

ROADMAP.name: The Trading Quest
ROADMAP.note: Start with a Yoshi Doll from the Trendy Game and trade it islander to islander, item by item. The final swap earns the Magnifying Lens, which reveals a hidden truth.

ROADMAP.name: Secret Seashells
ROADMAP.note: Hidden under bushes, in caves, and buried in the sand (use the Shovel). Collect enough and the Seashell Mansion rewards you with a far stronger sword.

ROADMAP.name: Pieces of Heart
ROADMAP.note: Twelve are scattered across Koholint. Every four add a full heart to your life meter.

TIPS.name: A dream you must end
items: Koholint Island is the dream of the sleeping Wind Fish. To go home, Link must wake it — by gathering the eight Instruments of the Sirens and playing the Ballad of the Wind Fish at the Egg on the mountain.
It's bittersweet on purpose: waking the Wind Fish means the island and everyone on it fade away. The game leans into that, so it's okay to slow down and enjoy Koholint first.
There's no Hyrule, no Triforce, and no Ganon here — just one island, one song, and a long sleep to break.

TIPS.name: Only two buttons
items: You can equip just TWO items at once (to A and B), so you'll swap constantly through the menu. Sword + Roc's Feather is the pair you'll use most early on.
Pegasus Boots (hold to dash) + Roc's Feather lets you long-jump wide gaps. The Power Bracelet lifts pots and rocks; the Hookshot crosses pits and stuns enemies.
Bombs and the Bow combine — drop a Bomb and shoot it, or 'bomb-arrow' tough foes for big damage. The Magic Rod burns enemies and lights torches.

TIPS.name: Three ocarina songs
items: You learn three melodies: the Ballad of the Wind Fish (the goal song), Manbo's Mambo (warps you to a set spot), and the Frog's Song of Soul (revives and powers certain things).
Owl statues across the island give hints when you hold the right item, and the Telephone Booths replay your current goal if you forget where to go.
Save often — the game can be paused/saved from the menu, and bottomless-pit falls or boss deaths send you back to the last save or dungeon entrance.

COLLECTIBLES.label: Pieces of Heart
COLLECTIBLES.note: Every 4 make a full Heart Container.

COLLECTIBLES.label: Secret Seashells
COLLECTIBLES.note: Hidden across Koholint; collect enough to claim a stronger sword at the Seashell Mansion.
